title: puccinellia palustris subsp. convoluta
intro: Puccinellia palustris subsp. convoluta, also known as Marsh Fescue or Saltmarsh Fescue, is a subspecies of grass that thrives in coastal and salt-marsh areas, characterized by its convolute leaf blades and a sprawling growth habit. Traditionally, Puccinellia palustris subsp. convoluta has been used in folk medicine, particularly in Russia and other parts of Eastern Europe, where it is valued for its adaptogenic properties and ability to reduce stress and fatigue. The plant contains flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including the alleviation of digestive issues and the support of immune function. In herbal preparations, Puccinellia palustris subsp. convoluta is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in combination with other herbs to create adaptogenic and stress-reducing remedies.
